Opening Scene — Claude obliges. In three minutes she has 800 words of professional academic prose, five paragraphs, nine citations with

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Route research and writing tasks to the right Claude surface
· Design a literature search workflow that Claude can support without substituting for
· Use Claude for manuscript development while preserving your accountability for every claim
· Plan quantitative and qualitative analysis with Claude's help without surrendering methodological judgment
· Build a simple AI-use log that satisfies disclosure expectations at most venues
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: THE CENTRAL DISTINCTION: SUPPORT VERSUS CLAIM
blocks: Research and writing consist of two categories of labor. Claude is well-suited to one and prohibited from the other.
The Central Distinction: Support Versus — Support work includes: brainstorming research questions, drafting search terms, organizing a source matrix, summarizing

data.title: RESEARCH WORKFLOW ROUTING.
blocks: Brainstorm research questions — Strong
Generate search terms — Strong
Summarize provided PDFs — Strong
Build a source matrix — Possible

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — LITERATURE SUPPORT: WHAT YOU C

data.title: LITERATURE SUPPORT: WHAT YOU CAN AND CANNOT DELEGATE
blocks: · Translate a research question into database search terms, synonyms, MeSH terms, or Boolean combinations
· Draft inclusion and exclusion criteria for a screening protocol
Create a structured source matrix (columns citation, method, sample, measure, finding, limitation, relevance)
· Summarize the argument and main finding of a PDF you supply
· Compare two papers on a specific dimension you name

data.title: WRITING SUPPORT: ASSISTANCE WITHOUT AUTHORSHIP
blocks: · Identifying unclear sentences, buried transitions, or missing signposts
· Generating multiple versions of a sentence so you can choose the clearest
· Drafting a section outline from a set of claims you provide
Acting as a skeptical reader "What objections could a reviewer raise to this argument?"
· Checking whether your conclusion actually follows from your stated evidence

data.title: ANALYSIS SUPPORT: PLANNING WITHOUT SURRENDER
blocks: · Knowing whether your measurement instrument is valid for your population
· Judging whether your sample size and design are adequate for causal claims
· Handling missing data decisions that depend on domain knowledge
· Deciding whether causal language is warranted or only associational
Interpreting a coefficient in context what does a two-point change on this scale mean for a real person?

S09.meta: // SLIDE 09 — PEER REVIEW AND MANUSCRIPT FEE

data.title: PEER REVIEW AND MANUSCRIPT FEEDBACK
blocks: Claude can act as a structured critical reader during revision: identifying weak transitions, unstated assumptions, poss
Peer Review and Manuscript Feedback — Claude should not be used for actual peer review of confidential manuscripts without understanding your journal's policy

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: "Claude can do the literature review for me."
"A citation in an AI output is probably real."
"If Claude found a theme, the theme is in the data."
"Language revision does not affect meaning."
"AI-generated peer feedback is objective."

data.title: THE CORE CLAIM.
